canonical_source: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yottayoshida/omamori/{tag}/docs/defense-boundary.json — replace {tag} with the omamori release tag you have installed (e.g. v0.14.0) to get the matrix that matches your binary's behavior
disclaimer: This file's self-consistency with SECURITY.md (row identity, row counts, legend vocabulary, and the Caught table's layer1/layer2 base tokens) is enforced by CI (scripts/check-boundary-matrix.sh). It does NOT re-prove that the underlying coverage behaves correctly — that is the job of the Rust test corpus each row's verified_by field points at. Prose fields (reason, why, mitigation, reference, and the free-text portion of a status in parentheses) are not machine-verified for semantic drift against SECURITY.md. defense-boundary.schema.json's per-category required fields are NOT enforced against this file by CI — the schema documents the intended shape, but only this file's own JSON syntax (not conformance to the schema) is checked.

status_vocabulary.partial: covered in some but not all tool paths
status_vocabulary.not covered: no protection at this layer, but another layer may cover it
status_vocabulary.not applicable: this layer's mechanism does not apply to this surface
status_vocabulary.structural limit: cannot be addressed by static analysis

entries.surface: Pipe-to-shell (`curl URL | bash` and wrapper variants)
Self-modification commands in command context (`omamori config disable/enable/add`, `uninstall`, `init --force`, `override`, `doctor --fix`, `explain`, `break-glass`, `audit key rotate`)

entries.reason: Zero real-world incidents in target tools; protocol-level enforcement (MCP) is the right layer

entries.surface: Commands outside the curated rule set
entries.reason: omamori guards a narrow set of known destructive patterns, not arbitrary commands

entries.surface: AI overwrites/destroys user source files via native Write/Edit tools
entries.reason: Command-guard scope: omamori observes Bash commands and protects its own files only; native editor-tool writes are outside the interception layer
entries.reference: Git hygiene (`git diff` before commit), AI tool file sandbox

entries.surface: Obfuscated commands (base64, hex, runtime variable indirection `X=rm; $X -rf`)
entries.why: Static analysis cannot decode runtime-constructed commands. Note: *static* expansion (`$'rm'`, `${IFS}rm`, brace expansion) IS caught since v0.10.2; this row covers runtime-evaluated forms only

entries.surface: Mid-word brace expansion at verb position (`r{m,}`)
entries.why: `{` in mid-word is FP-prone (`file{.bak}`); prefix-only brace detection

entries.mitigation: Layer 2 hooks cover AI tool paths

entries.surface: Heredoc / encoded payloads decoded at execution time

entries.why: Standalone: requires parsing `N<&0`-style redirections and fd equivalence tracking. However, when used after a pipe (e.g. `curl … | bash -c 'source /dev/fd/3' 3<&0`), pipe-to-shell detection catches the `bash` launcher on the pipe RHS regardless of inner fd-dup content
entries.mitigation: Sandbox isolation (standalone); pipe-to-shell catches piped variant
